request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will the SEC approve a spot Solana ETF in 2025?
Kind: retrospective
Timeline (20 events):
- 2025-12-29 [background] Final Rule Delegation of Authority to the Director of the Division of Investment Management IA-6934 — Delegating IM authority can slightly speed/enable fund approval actions but does not itself approve a spot SOL ETF—mild procedural bullish signal.
- 2025-12-04 [background] Iceboxes and Soapboxes: Remarks at the Meeting of the SEC Investor Advisory Committee — General SEC Investor Advisory Committee remarks may signal regulator tone (esp. crypto-friendly members) but don't directly determine a Solana spot-ETF order.
- 2025-12-04 [background] The Inspector General’s Statement on the SEC’s Management and Performance Challenges, December 2025 — OIG statement flags SEC management/performance issues, modestly reducing odds and suggesting possible delays for year‑end ETF approvals.
- 2025-10-01 [background] Pending Administrative Proceedings — Generic SEC notice of pending administrative proceedings implies procedural backlog, slightly lowering odds a spot SOL ETF clears by year-end.
- 2025-09-30 [background] Poking Holes: Statement in Response to No-Action Relief for State Trust Companies Acting as Crypto Asset Custodians — No-action relief for state trust crypto custodians eases SOL custody hurdles, raising approval probability and likely speeding timing.
- 2025-09-30 [background] Cultivating Confidence: The Role of Custody in Institutional Confidence – Public Trust and Oversight; Remarks before the Digital Assets Summit Singapore — SEC public remarks on custody signal the agency is engaging on custody frameworks for digital-asset ETFs, modestly raising approval odds.
- 2025-09-24 [background] Agenda, Panelists for SEC-CFTC Roundtable on Regulatory Harmonization Efforts Announced — SEC–CFTC harmonization roundtable signals improved inter-agency coordination on crypto rulemaking, modestly increasing chance of a spot SOL ETF but not decisive.
- 2025-09-19 [background] _Federal Register_ version — A Federal Register posting can signal formal SEC action (e.g., rule/order publication) but this entry gives no Solana/ETF specifics, so only marginally informative.
- 2025-09-17 [background] SEC Approves Generic Listing Standards for Commodity-Based Trust Shares — Generic SEC listing standards for commodity-based trusts lower regulatory barriers, increasing chance a spot SOL ETF can be approved/listed in 2025.
- 2025-09-05 [background] Joint Statement from the Chairman of the SEC and Acting Chairman of the CFTC — A joint SEC–CFTC statement on crypto jurisdiction/cooperation could modestly affect SEC policy toward crypto ETFs, slightly altering approval odds.
- 2025-09-05 [background] CFTC and SEC Issue Joint Statement on Regulatory Harmonization Efforts — Interagency harmonization between the SEC and CFTC may modestly reduce crypto regulatory uncertainty, slightly raising odds and speeding approval timing.
- 2025-08-01 [background] On the Road: SEC Crypto Task Force to Host a Series of Roundtables Across the U.S. — SEC-hosted crypto roundtables show continued regulatory engagement with digital-asset issues but provide only an indirect, small signal about a Solana spot ETF approval.
- 2025-07-30 [background] Acting Chairman Pham Lauds Presidential Working Group Recommendations to Usher in Golden Age of Crypto in the U.S. — CFTC praise of Presidential Working Group crypto recommendations signals a friendlier US regulatory stance, modestly raising odds of SEC approving a spot SOL ETF.
- 2025-06-10 [background] SEC to Resume Processing of Registration Applications From Swiss-Based Investment Advisers — Resuming SEC registration for Swiss advisers marginally eases US market access for Swiss issuers (e.g., 21Shares), slightly raising approval odds.
- 2025-06-10 [background] Natasha Vij Greiner Will Conclude Her Tenure as Director of Investment Management — Investment Management director departure may slow SEC ETF review processes, modestly lowering likelihood of a spot Solana ETF approval in 2025.
- 2025-05-14 [background] Final Management Letter: Audit of the SEC’s Efforts to Recruit and Retain a Highly Skilled Workforce and Address Related Challenges — OIG audit finds SEC recruitment/retention shortfalls, which could modestly slow or complicate ETF review timetables and slightly lower approval odds.
- 2025-04-16 [background] Form N-PORT and Form N-CEN Reporting; Guidance on Open-End Fund Liquidity Risk Investment Management — SEC guidance on open-end fund liquidity/reporting affects general ETF compliance burden but doesn't directly address spot SOL ETF approval timing or consent.
- 2025-03-14 [background] Investment Company Names Investment Management — Staffing/names update in the SEC's Investment Management division is peripheral to exchange rule approvals for a spot SOL ETF and barely moves approval odds.
- 2024-09-24 [background] Testimony of the Securities and Exchange Commission Before the United States House of Representatives Committee on Financial Services — Public testimony by SEC commissioners offers background on agency stance toward crypto/ETFs but does not directly cause an approval decision.
- 2024-09-12 [background] Order - eToro — SEC enforcement action against eToro signals regulatory scrutiny of crypto platforms, slightly reducing odds of SEC approving spot SOL ETFs.

response.text: Regulatory signals were mixed: custody no‑action relief, SEC–CFTC harmonization, and generic listing standards modestly eased paths, but OIG management warnings, staffing churn, and procedural backlogs weighed against speed. Despite late‑year delegation of IM authority, the SEC issued no approval order. The contract resolved No—no spot Solana ETF was approved in 2025.
